beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee at home while checking work emails and the weather forecast.
beats.reasoning: Low energy yesterday (40/100) means a slower start; Ethan wakes to grey drizzle and knows SR-520 closure will complicate the Tukwila commute, so he takes time to plan the route and mentally prep for a rain day.

beats.activity: Drive to Tukwila optometry practice via I-5 South, avoiding the SR-520 closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Ethan routes south on I-5 to avoid the snarl, leaving early to arrive before the closure window hits other arterials.

beats.activity: Morning patient appointments: eye exams, frame fittings, and refraction work.
beats.reasoning: Steady morning shift; rainy weather keeps foot traffic lighter than usual, but Ethan stays focused on patient care and the routine diagnostic work that defines his practice.

beats.activity: Lunch break: grab a quick sandwich and coffee at a nearby cafe, stay dry indoors.
beats.reasoning: Rain is still steady at noon; Ethan eats locally rather than venturing out, keeping the day simple and staying warm and dry.

beats.activity: Afternoon patient appointments and administrative work.
beats.reasoning: Rain tapers off after 2pm; Ethan completes the afternoon patient schedule, finishing his day on time without rushing.

beats.activity: Drive back to Seattle via I-5 North; SR-520 closure is now lifted, but Ethan sticks with I-5 for predictability.
beats.reasoning: By late afternoon the SR-520 closure is over, but Ethan is tired and doesn't want to risk unexpected traffic; he takes the familiar route home.

beats.activity: Stop at a local market to pick up groceries and fresh ingredients for dinner.
beats.reasoning: Rainy day and low energy carry over; Ethan grabs essentials for a simple home-cooked meal rather than eating out.

beats.activity: Cook dinner at home and eat while reviewing photos from yesterday's Pike Place shoot on the computer.
beats.reasoning: Ethan decompresses after a steady work day; cooking and reviewing his film-camera shots helps him transition from work mode and recharge before evening leisure.

diary: Gray morning in Ballard—coffee tasted better than usual, maybe because the rain was coming down so steady. Checked the emails while the weather app lit up red; SR-520 was already a mess before I even left the house. Smart to stick with I-5 South; got to the practice without drama, which felt like a small victory. The morning flew by—frames, refraction, the usual gentle back-and-forth with patients. There's something grounding about helping someone see clearly again. Grabbed a sandwich at Tukwila Market and just sat there, watching the rain streak the windows. Afternoon was packed. By the time I locked up, my shoulders were tight. The drive back felt longer than usual, but I-5 moved okay. Stopped at Ballard Market on the way home—picked up salmon and asparagus, something fresh. Cooked while scrolling through yesterday's Pike Place photos on the computer. Those shots turned out better than I expected. Ate alone, rain still tapping the windows. Tired now, but it's a good tired.
thought_process: The weather forecast this morning made me cautious—that SR-520 closure was guaranteed gridlock, so I committed to I-5 early instead of second-guessing myself later. The rain kept the practice quieter than some Tuesdays, which I actually appreciated; fewer cancellations than I'd feared. At lunch, I needed stillness more than speed, so the deli worked. Afternoon felt long—administrative work always drags when the weather's this bleak—but I pushed through. On the drive back, I noticed the closure was lifted but didn't bother switching routes; predictability mattered more today than saving ten minutes. Stopping at the market felt necessary, a small ritual. The Pike Place photos on my computer pulled me out of the workday funk—reminded me I have life outside the practice. That shift from work-tired to something more satisfied happened while I was cooking, which is why I lingered over dinner.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
